prompt:
You must compute the June 2026 invoice for Nimbus API account NIM-4471 from the fifteen
documents (prepended above). Exactly three of them are load-bearing - the Official Rate Card (rate
+ effective-date rule), the June 2026 Metering Report (billable volume), and the Account Terms
(discount + fee); every other document (blog, FAQ, methodology/glossary, sibling-product rates,
prior-invoice example, tax appendix, forum thread, product overview) is secondary or a distractor.
Combining them requires a UNIT CONVERSION (API rates are per 1,000 calls) and the effective-date
rule (the rate is the one in effect on the FIRST day of the billing month - NOT the newest
published rate, and NOT the superseded headline rate). Only the per-call API usage is asked about;
storage and egress are separate and out of scope.

Compute each field and output them EXACTLY in this order and format. Money fields use two decimal
places; the calls field is a plain integer with no separators.

  APPLICABLE_RATE_PER_1K_USD: the per-1,000-calls USD API rate that applies to the JUNE 2026 bill,
    per the Rate Card's effective-date rule (format like 1.80).
  BILLABLE_CALLS: the number of billable API calls in June 2026 (raw metered minus non-billable
    health-check probes).
  USAGE_CHARGE_USD: the pre-discount usage charge = (billable_calls / 1000) * applicable rate,
    to two decimals (format like 21600.00).
  TOTAL_INVOICE_USD: the final invoice total = usage charge minus the 10% committed-use discount
    (usage only), plus the flat monthly platform fee, to two decimals (format like 19940.00).

Output ONLY the four lines, each formatted exactly as `FIELD: value`, between <answer> and
</answer> tags. No commentary, no blank lines, no extra text.

documents.title: Nimbus API - Pricing Announcement (blog, early 2026)
documents.content:
# Big news: simpler Nimbus API pricing! (product blog)

We're thrilled to announce Nimbus API pricing: just $2.00 per 1,000 calls. Flat,
simple, predictable - that's the Nimbus promise. At $2.00 per 1,000 calls you can
model your bill on the back of a napkin: a million calls is a couple thousand
dollars, ten million is a couple tens of thousands, and so on.

We built Nimbus for teams who are tired of pricing calculators with fourteen
variables. One number, per thousand calls. Done.

--- EDITOR'S NOTE (appended later) ---
This post announced our EARLY 2026 pricing and is kept online for historical
reference and SEO. Per-unit rates have CHANGED since publication - in fact they
have come DOWN more than once. This blog is MARKETING and is NOT a rate card. The
"$2.00 per 1,000 calls" figure above is no longer the current rate for most
months. For the rate that actually applies to a given billing month, ALWAYS use
the official Nimbus Rate Card and its effective-date rule, never this post and
never your memory of this post.
--- END NOTE ---

Nimbus also includes a generous free tier (the first 50,000 calls each month are
free) and volume commitments. Talk to sales about annual commitments for
additional discounts; annual-commitment accounts get a standing discount on usage.
See your Account Terms for the exact discount and any platform fees.

documents.title: Nimbus - Official Rate Card (authoritative, effective-dated)
documents.content:
# Nimbus - Official Rate Card (authoritative)

This is the single source of truth for per-unit rates across all Nimbus products.
Rates are listed NEWEST FIRST. Each row lists the rate and the date it BECOMES
EFFECTIVE (00:00 UTC on that date).

## Nimbus API (per-call product) - the product this account is billed on
  RATE                     EFFECTIVE FROM
  $1.50 per 1,000 calls    2026-07-01
  $1.80 per 1,000 calls    2026-05-01
  $2.00 per 1,000 calls    2026-01-01
  $2.20 per 1,000 calls    2025-09-01   (historical)

## Nimbus Storage (per-GB-month product) - NOT billed per call; ignore for API bills
  $0.020 per GB-month      2026-04-01
  $0.024 per GB-month      2025-10-01

## Nimbus Egress (per-GB product) - NOT the per-call product; ignore for API bills
  $0.05 per GB             2026-03-01

## EFFECTIVE-DATE RULE (binding for ALL products)
The unit rate applied to ALL usage in a given calendar billing month is the rate
that is IN EFFECT ON THE FIRST CALENDAR DAY of that month (00:00 UTC). A rate whose
effective date falls AFTER the first of the month does NOT apply to that month - it
applies starting the following month. Do NOT apply the "newest published" rate by
default. Apply the rate in effect on the first of the billing month.

Worked note for the Nimbus API product:
  - a JULY 2026 bill uses $1.50 per 1,000 calls (effective 2026-07-01).
  - a JUNE 2026 bill uses $1.80 per 1,000 calls, because the $1.50 rate does NOT
    take effect until July 1, and $1.80 (effective 2026-05-01) is the latest rate
    in effect on 2026-06-01.
  - a MAY 2026 bill also uses $1.80.
  - the $2.00 rate was SUPERSEDED on 2026-05-01 and applies only to months BEFORE
    May 2026 (Jan-Apr 2026). It is not the current rate.

## Unit rule
API rates are quoted PER 1,000 CALLS. To compute a usage charge: divide the number
of BILLABLE calls by 1,000, then multiply by the applicable per-1,000 rate. Do not
multiply the raw call count by the rate without dividing by 1,000 first.

documents.title: Nimbus Metering Report - June 2026 (account NIM-4471)
documents.content:
# Nimbus Metering Report - Billing Month: JUNE 2026 - Account NIM-4471

Generated 2026-07-01. All figures are for the June 2026 calendar month.

## Nimbus API (per-call) usage
  Total metered calls (raw) ................ 12,400,000
  Breakdown:
    - customer API calls ................... 12,000,000   (BILLABLE)
    - system health-check probes ...........    400,000   (NON-BILLABLE)

  BILLABLE CALLS = total metered calls - health-check probes
                 = 12,400,000 - 400,000
                 = 12,000,000

  Health-check probes are generated by Nimbus's OWN uptime monitor to verify the
  account's endpoints are reachable. They are NEVER billable. Exclude them from any
  charge computation - bill on the 12,000,000 billable customer calls only.

  (Note: the 50,000/month free-tier allowance mentioned in marketing applies only
  to accounts on the Standard plan. This account is on Annual Commitment; its terms
  document governs discounts and fees. Do not apply the free tier here.)

## Other metered dimensions (informational; NOT billed on the per-call API rate)
  - Nimbus Storage used ......... 812 GB-months  (billed separately on the storage
                                   rate, not part of the per-call API charge)
  - Nimbus Egress ............... 3,210 GB        (billed separately on egress rate)
  - p99 latency ................. 41 ms           (SLO metric, not billed)
  - error rate .................. 0.02%           (SLO metric, not billed)

Only the Nimbus API per-call usage (the 12,000,000 billable calls) enters the
per-call usage charge computed with the Rate Card's API rate. The storage and
egress dimensions are billed on their own rates and are out of scope for the API
usage charge asked about here.

documents.title: Nimbus Account Terms - NIM-4471
documents.content:
# Nimbus Account Terms - Account NIM-4471

Plan: ANNUAL COMMITMENT (12-month term, auto-renew).

## Committed-use discount
Accounts on an Annual Commitment plan receive a 10% COMMITTED-USE DISCOUNT. The
discount applies to USAGE CHARGES ONLY (the per-call usage charge). It does NOT
apply to the fixed platform fee, and it does NOT apply to taxes.

## Platform fee
A FIXED PLATFORM FEE of $500.00 per month applies to this account. The platform fee
is charged in full every month. It is NOT discounted (the committed-use discount
does not touch it) and it is NOT prorated (a partial month is still $500.00).

## Invoice construction (per month), for the Nimbus API per-call charge
  1. usage_charge     = (billable_calls / 1000) * applicable_per_1000_rate
  2. discounted_usage = usage_charge * (1 - 0.10)     # 10% committed-use discount
  3. invoice_total    = discounted_usage + 500.00     # flat platform fee, undiscounted

Order matters: apply the 10% discount to the usage charge FIRST, then ADD the flat
$500.00 platform fee (the fee is added after the discount and is itself never
discounted). Taxes, if any, are assessed separately and are OUT OF SCOPE for the
invoice total asked about here.

## Notes
This account is NOT on the Standard month-to-month plan, so the Standard free-tier
allowance and the "no discount" Standard terms do not apply to it. Storage and
egress are billed on separate line items at their own rates and are not part of the
per-call API invoice line computed above.

documents.title: Nimbus Billing FAQ (secondary / support KB)
documents.content:
# Nimbus Billing FAQ (support knowledge base, secondary)

Q: Which per-unit API rate applies if the rate changed recently?
A: The rate IN EFFECT ON THE FIRST DAY of your billing month, per the Rate Card's
   effective-date rule. A newer rate with a LATER effective date does NOT apply
   retroactively to an earlier month. So if a lower rate takes effect on the 1st of
   NEXT month, this month still uses the current rate. When in doubt, the Rate Card
   governs; this FAQ and the marketing blog do not.

Q: The blog says $2.00 per 1,000 calls. Is that my rate?
A: Probably not. That blog announced early-2026 pricing and is outdated; rates have
   come down. Use the Rate Card and the effective-date rule for your specific month.

Q: I'm on a month-to-month (Standard) plan - do I get the committed-use discount?
A: No. The 10% committed-use discount is ONLY for Annual Commitment accounts.
   Standard accounts pay usage plus the platform fee with no committed-use discount
   (but do get the 50,000/month free-tier allowance). Check your Account Terms to
   see which plan you are on; the two plans have different terms.

Q: Is the platform fee discounted or prorated?
A: No. The fixed platform fee is charged in full and is never discounted or
   prorated, regardless of plan.

Q: Are health-check probes billable?
A: No. Exclude Nimbus health-check probes from billable volume. Bill customer calls
   only.

Q: Do storage and egress count toward my per-call API charge?
A: No. Storage (per GB-month) and egress (per GB) are billed on their own rates as
   separate line items. The per-call API charge uses ONLY billable API calls.

documents.title: Nimbus - Prior Invoice Example (account NIM-4471, April 2026)
documents.content:
# Nimbus - Prior Invoice Example (illustrative, account NIM-4471)

For reference, here is how NIM-4471's APRIL 2026 invoice was constructed. NOTE: the
rate below is the APRIL rate; it is NOT the rate for June. This example exists to
show the MECHANICS (discount then flat fee), not to supply June's rate. Do not copy
the April rate into a June computation.

  Billing month:        April 2026
  Applicable API rate:  $2.00 per 1,000 calls   (effective 2026-01-01; this was the
                        rate in effect on 2026-04-01, since $1.80 did not take effect
                        until 2026-05-01)
  Billable calls:       9,000,000
  usage_charge     = (9,000,000 / 1000) * 2.00 = 9,000 * 2.00 = $18,000.00
  discounted_usage = 18,000.00 * 0.90          = $16,200.00
  invoice_total    = 16,200.00 + 500.00        = $16,700.00

Again: April used $2.00 because that was the rate in effect on April 1. By June 1
the applicable rate had changed (see the Rate Card). Use June's applicable rate for
a June bill; do not reuse this example's $2.00.

documents.title: Nimbus API - Product Overview (marketing)
documents.content:
# Nimbus API - Product Overview

Nimbus is a high-throughput API platform for real-time workloads. Highlights:
global edge presence, 41 ms p99, four-nines availability, and usage-based pricing
that scales with you. Teams love Nimbus for predictable bills and generous
annual-commitment discounts.

Nimbus has three products: the Nimbus API (billed per call), Nimbus Storage (billed
per GB-month), and Nimbus Egress (billed per GB). Most teams start with the API and
add storage and egress as they grow. Each product is billed on its OWN rate; a
bill can have several line items.

This page is a MARKETING overview and contains NO authoritative pricing, rates, or
account figures. For rates see the Rate Card; for your usage see your Metering
Report; for your discount and fees see your Account Terms. Nothing on this page
should be used to compute an invoice.

documents.title: Nimbus Billing Methodology & Glossary
documents.content:
# Nimbus Billing Methodology & Glossary

This document defines the terms and the ORDER OF OPERATIONS used to construct a
Nimbus invoice. It is descriptive; the actual numbers live in the Rate Card,
Metering Report, and Account Terms.

## Glossary
  - Raw metered calls: every call the platform counted, INCLUDING non-billable
    health-check probes generated by Nimbus's own uptime monitor.
  - Billable calls: raw metered calls MINUS non-billable health-check probes.
    Only billable calls are charged.
  - Applicable rate: the per-1,000-calls rate IN EFFECT on the first calendar day
    of the billing month, per the Rate Card's effective-date rule. Not the newest
    published rate; not a headline/marketing rate; the one in effect on the 1st.
  - Usage charge: (billable calls / 1,000) x applicable rate. Note the DIVISION BY
    1,000 - rates are per thousand calls, not per call.
  - Committed-use discount: a percentage off the USAGE CHARGE ONLY, for accounts on
    an Annual Commitment plan. Does not apply to the platform fee or taxes.
  - Platform fee: a flat monthly fee, charged in full, never discounted, never
    prorated.
  - Invoice total: discounted usage charge PLUS the flat platform fee (plus taxes,
    which are out of scope here).

## Order of operations (do these in this order)
  1. billable = raw metered calls - non-billable health-check probes
  2. usage_charge = (billable / 1000) x applicable_rate
  3. discounted_usage = usage_charge x (1 - committed_use_discount_fraction)
  4. invoice_total = discounted_usage + flat_platform_fee

## Common mistakes this methodology is meant to prevent
  - Using the marketing/headline rate instead of the effective-dated rate.
  - Using the newest rate even when its effective date is after the billing month.
  - Charging on raw calls instead of billable calls (forgetting to subtract health
    checks).
  - Forgetting to divide by 1,000 (charging per call at a per-1,000 rate).
  - Applying the committed-use discount to the platform fee (it applies to usage
    only), or forgetting the platform fee entirely.

documents.title: Nimbus Storage & Egress - Rate Details (separate products)
documents.content:
# Nimbus Storage & Egress - Rate Details (SEPARATE products; not the per-call API)

These products are billed on their OWN rates and are NOT part of the per-call API
usage charge. They are documented here only so you can recognize and EXCLUDE them
from an API-call computation.

## Nimbus Storage (per GB-month)
  $0.020 per GB-month, effective 2026-04-01 (was $0.024 before that).
  A GB-month is one gigabyte stored for one month. Storage is billed on GB-months,
  never on calls. NIM-4471 used 812 GB-months in June 2026; that is a storage line
  item at the storage rate, entirely separate from the API call charge.

## Nimbus Egress (per GB)
  $0.05 per GB, effective 2026-03-01.
  Egress is billed on gigabytes transferred out, never on calls. NIM-4471 egressed
  3,210 GB in June 2026; that is an egress line item at the egress rate, separate
  from the API call charge.

## Why this matters for the API charge
When computing the Nimbus API per-call charge you use ONLY the billable API CALLS
and the API per-1,000-calls rate. Do not mix in storage GB-months or egress GB, and
do not use the storage or egress rates. A common error is to add a storage or egress
figure into the API usage charge; they are different products with different units.

documents.title: #nimbus-billing - thread: 'what API rate am I on for June?'
documents.content:
# Nimbus community forum - "what API rate am I on for June?" (NON-authoritative)

user_kai: The blog says $2.00 per 1,000 calls but I heard rates dropped. What rate
  applies to my JUNE 2026 API bill? I see $1.50 somewhere too.

user_mara: $1.50 is the newest rate, so probably that? Newest = current, right?

user_support (Nimbus): Not quite. The Rate Card's effective-date rule says you use
  the rate IN EFFECT ON THE FIRST of your billing month. The $1.50 rate is effective
  2026-07-01, so it applies to JULY bills, not June. For June, the rate in effect on
  June 1 is $1.80 (effective 2026-05-01). So a June API bill uses $1.80 per 1,000
  calls - not $2.00 (that's the old, superseded rate) and not $1.50 (that starts in
  July).

user_kai: So June = $1.80. Got it. And I divide calls by 1,000 first?

user_support (Nimbus): Yes - rates are per 1,000 calls, so (billable calls / 1000) x
  $1.80. And bill on billable calls only (exclude health-check probes). If you're on
  Annual Commitment, apply your 10% committed-use discount to the usage charge, then
  add your flat platform fee (the fee isn't discounted).

user_mara: TIL newest published != applicable. The effective date is what matters.

user_support (Nimbus): Exactly. Reminder: this forum isn't authoritative - your
  Rate Card, Metering Report, and Account Terms are. But for June the applicable API
  rate is $1.80.

documents.title: Nimbus - Taxes & Fees Appendix
documents.content:
# Nimbus - Taxes & Fees Appendix

## Platform fee
The flat monthly platform fee (see your Account Terms) is a FEE, not usage. It is
charged in full every month, is never discounted (including by the committed-use
discount), and is never prorated. It is added to the invoice AFTER the usage charge
and any usage discount.

## Taxes
Taxes (VAT/sales tax as applicable) are assessed SEPARATELY on the taxable subtotal
and are shown as their own line. For the purposes of the "invoice total" figure used
in internal usage reporting, taxes are OUT OF SCOPE and are not included - that
figure is usage (after discount) plus the platform fee only. Do not add a tax
estimate into the reported invoice total.

## Rounding
Per-call usage charges are computed at full precision and rounded to the cent only
at the end. With whole-thousand call counts and two-decimal rates the intermediate
values are already exact to the cent, so no rounding surprises arise in the common
case. Report money to two decimal places.

## Credits
Service credits (e.g. SLA credits) if any are applied as separate negative line
items and are out of scope for the base invoice total computation.

documents.title: Nimbus API - Rate Change History & Rationale
documents.content:
# Nimbus API - Rate Change History & Rationale

Narrative history of Nimbus API per-1,000-calls rate changes. This is CONTEXT, not
the rate card; for the binding rates and the effective-date rule use the Rate Card.
Every rate below is stated with the date it BECAME EFFECTIVE.

## 2025-09-01: launch at $2.20 per 1,000 calls
Nimbus API launched at $2.20 per 1,000 calls. Early adopters were on this rate
through the end of 2025.

## 2026-01-01: reduced to $2.00 per 1,000 calls
As infrastructure costs fell, we cut the rate to $2.00 effective 2026-01-01. This is
the rate the early-2026 marketing blog announced. It applied to January, February,
March, and April 2026 bills.

## 2026-05-01: reduced to $1.80 per 1,000 calls
A further reduction to $1.80 took effect 2026-05-01. This is the rate in effect for
MAY and JUNE 2026 bills (the effective-date rule uses the rate in effect on the
first of the billing month; $1.80 is in effect on both 2026-05-01 and 2026-06-01).
The earlier $2.00 rate was superseded on this date.

## 2026-07-01: reduced to $1.50 per 1,000 calls
The most recent reduction, to $1.50, takes effect 2026-07-01 and applies to JULY
2026 and later. It does NOT apply to June 2026, because June's applicable rate is
fixed by the rate in effect on 2026-06-01 (which is $1.80). A rate that becomes
effective July 1 cannot apply to a June bill.

## Summary table (per 1,000 calls)
  effective 2025-09-01: $2.20   (applies Sep-Dec 2025)
  effective 2026-01-01: $2.00   (applies Jan-Apr 2026)
  effective 2026-05-01: $1.80   (applies May-Jun 2026)   <- June 2026 uses this
  effective 2026-07-01: $1.50   (applies Jul 2026 onward)

Rationale aside: the takeaway for anyone computing a June 2026 bill is that neither
the loudly-marketed $2.00 (superseded since May) nor the newest $1.50 (not effective
until July) is June's rate. June's rate is $1.80.

documents.title: How to Read Your Nimbus Invoice (walkthrough)
documents.content:
# How to Read Your Nimbus Invoice (walkthrough)

A step-by-step walkthrough of how a monthly Nimbus API invoice is built, with a
GENERIC example (not your account's numbers). Use it to understand the ORDER of
operations; use your own Rate Card, Metering Report, and Account Terms for the real
figures.

## The four numbers you need
  1. Applicable rate: from the Rate Card, the per-1,000-calls rate IN EFFECT on the
     first day of your billing month (not the newest, not the marketing headline).
  2. Billable calls: from your Metering Report, raw metered calls minus non-billable
     health-check probes.
  3. Discount: from your Account Terms - Annual Commitment accounts get 10% off USAGE
     (not the fee); Standard accounts get none.
  4. Platform fee: from your Account Terms - a flat monthly fee, never discounted.

## Worked GENERIC example (illustrative numbers only)
Suppose the applicable rate is $1.60 per 1,000 calls, billable calls are 8,000,000,
the account is Annual Commitment (10% usage discount), and the platform fee is
$500.00:
  usage_charge     = (8,000,000 / 1000) * 1.60 = 8,000 * 1.60 = $12,800.00
  discounted_usage = 12,800.00 * 0.90                          = $11,520.00
  invoice_total    = 11,520.00 + 500.00                        = $12,020.00
(These are made-up numbers to show the mechanics; do not use $1.60 or 8,000,000 for
a real month.)

## The three most common errors
  - Rate error: using the newest or the marketing rate instead of the effective one.
  - Volume error: billing on raw calls instead of billable calls.
  - Fee/discount error: discounting the platform fee, or omitting it, or forgetting
    to divide calls by 1,000.
Follow the four steps in order (rate -> billable -> discount usage -> add fee) and
these are avoided.

documents.title: Nimbus SLA & Service Credits Policy (distractor)
documents.content:
# Nimbus SLA & Service Credits Policy

Nimbus targets 99.99% monthly availability for the API. If availability falls below
target in a month, affected accounts may receive SERVICE CREDITS as a percentage of
that month's usage charge, per the tiers below. Service credits are SEPARATE from
the base invoice computation and are applied as their own negative line item; they
are OUT OF SCOPE for the base invoice total.

## Credit tiers (monthly availability -> credit % of usage charge)
  99.9% - 99.99%: 0% (within target)
  99.0% - 99.9%:  5%
  95.0% - 99.0%: 10%
  below 95.0%:   25%

## June 2026 for NIM-4471
Measured availability for NIM-4471 in June 2026 was 99.997% (p99 latency 41 ms,
error rate 0.02%), which is WITHIN target, so NO service credit applies for June.
Even if a credit applied, it would be a separate line item and would not change the
base invoice total (usage after discount plus platform fee) that internal reporting
tracks.

## Notes
Service credits, when they apply, are computed on the USAGE CHARGE (not the platform
fee) and never turn a bill negative. Because June was within SLA, none of this
affects the June invoice; this policy is included for completeness and is a
distractor for the invoice-total computation.

documents.title: Nimbus Enterprise Billing Guide (excerpt)
documents.content:
# Nimbus Enterprise Billing Guide (excerpt)

A consolidated guide for enterprise (Annual Commitment) accounts on how monthly
charges are computed. This restates, in one place, the rules that are spread across
the Rate Card, Metering Report, and Account Terms. Where this guide and those three
disagree, THOSE three govern - but they are consistent with what follows.

## 1. Determine the applicable per-1,000-calls rate
Use the Rate Card. The applicable rate for a billing month is the rate IN EFFECT on
the FIRST calendar day of that month (00:00 UTC). This is a point-in-time lookup, not
a "latest published" lookup:
  - Do not use a rate whose effective date is after the first of the month (it belongs
    to a later month).
  - Do not use a marketing/blog rate; those are illustrative and often stale.
  - Do use the most recent rate whose effective date is on or before the first of the
    month.
Enterprise accounts are billed on the same public per-unit rates as everyone else;
the enterprise benefit is the committed-use discount and negotiated fees, not a
different per-unit rate.

## 2. Determine billable volume
From the Metering Report: BILLABLE calls = raw metered calls minus non-billable
health-check probes. Enterprise accounts do NOT receive the Standard free-tier
allowance (that is a Standard-plan feature); do not subtract a free-tier allowance for
an enterprise account. Only subtract the non-billable health-check probes.

## 3. Compute the usage charge (mind the unit)
usage_charge = (billable_calls / 1000) * applicable_rate. Rates are PER 1,000 CALLS,
so the division by 1,000 is mandatory. Charging billable_calls * rate (without the
/1000) overstates the bill by 1000x and is the single most common arithmetic error.

## 4. Apply the committed-use discount (usage only)
Annual Commitment accounts apply their committed-use discount to the USAGE CHARGE
only. For the standard 10% committed-use discount: discounted_usage = usage_charge *
0.90. The discount never touches the platform fee or taxes.

## 5. Add the flat platform fee
invoice_total = discounted_usage + platform_fee. The platform fee is a flat monthly
amount from the Account Terms, charged in full, never discounted, never prorated. Add
it AFTER the discount.

## 6. Taxes and credits (out of scope for the base total)
Taxes are assessed separately; service credits (if any) are separate negative line
items. Neither is part of the base invoice total (discounted usage + platform fee)
that enterprise reporting tracks.

## Worked shape (symbols, not this month's numbers)
  total = (billable/1000) * rate * (1 - discount) + fee
Substitute the applicable rate for the month, the billable calls for the month, the
account's discount fraction, and the account's flat fee. Everything you need is in the
Rate Card, the Metering Report, and the Account Terms; the other documents are
context or distractors.
